name: Six Layer Coffee Torte
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour 3 8-inch round cake pans. In a large bowl, measure first 9 ingredients. With mixer on low speed, beat ingredients until just mixed, scraping the bowl constantly with a spatula. Dissolve 2 teaspoons instant coffee into 1 cup boiling water; add to the ingredients in the bowl. Increase mixer speed to medium and beat for 2 minutes, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally. Pour batter into pans. Bake for 25-30 minutes. Cool cake in pans on racks for 10 minutes. Remove from pans and cool completely. While the cake is cooling, finely chop Heath Bars. In a cup dissolve 1/2 teaspoon instant coffee in 1 teaspoon hot water; let it cool. With a serrated knife, cut each cake layer in half to make 6 thin layers. In a large bowl, beat whipping cream, brown sugar and coffee until stiff peaks form.
To assemble the cake, place 1 layer on a cake plate; spread with about 1/2 cup of the whipped cream mixture. Reserve about 2/3 of the chopped Heath Bars; sprinkle this layer with about 1/5 of the remaining Heath Bars. Repeat layering 4 more times. Top with the remaining cake layer. Thinly spread the remaining whipped cream mixture over the side and top of the cake. Gently press the reserved chopped Heath Bars onto the top and sides. Refrigerate the cake until ready to serve.
Serves 20.
